channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Global oil and energy prices are reeling as the war in the Middle East escalates
item.description: Israel and Iran continue to exchange airstrikes with a focus on energy infrastructure. President Trump says Israel acted alone in striking a key gas field and better not do it again.
item.content:encoded: Israel and Iran continue to exchange airstrikes with a focus on energy infrastructure. President Trump says Israel acted alone in striking a key gas field and better not do it again.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Kahn

item.title: Gas analyst shares how attacks on Middle East gas fields might affect energy markets
item.description: How might attacks on gas fields in the Middle East affect global energy markets? NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to Ira Joseph, a gas analyst at Columbia University's Center for Global Energy Policy.
item.content:encoded: How might attacks on gas fields in the Middle East affect global energy markets? NPR's Leila Fadel speaks to Ira Joseph, a gas analyst at Columbia University's Center for Global Energy Policy.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Trump's mediators offer Hamas a formal proposal to give up its weapons in Gaza
item.description: President Trump's Board of Peace has quietly given Hamas a proposal to hand over all of its weapons to ensure Gaza's reconstruction.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's Board of Peace has quietly given Hamas a proposal to hand over all of its weapons to ensure Gaza's reconstruction.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Global oil and energy prices reel as the war in the Middle East escalates, Trump talks about potential for ground troops in Iran and the possibility of seizing Kharg Island.
item.content:encoded: Global oil and energy prices reel as the war in the Middle East escalates, Trump talks about potential for ground troops in Iran and the possibility of seizing Kharg Island.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Lisa Kudrow talks about the upcoming season of her series 'The Comeback'
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with actress Lisa Kudrow about season 3 of her HBO series, "The Comeback," which returns Sunday.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with actress Lisa Kudrow about season 3 of her HBO series, "The Comeback," which returns Sunday.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: A look at the economic cost of the war in the Middle East
item.description: The team from NPR's The Indicator podcast takes a look at the economic costs of the war in the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: The team from NPR's The Indicator podcast takes a look at the economic costs of the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Darian Woods

item.title: Historian talks about how Trump is forging a new world order
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with historian Daniel Immerwahr about how President Trump is forging a new world order through his foreign policy.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with historian Daniel Immerwahr about how President Trump is forging a new world order through his foreign policy.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: ABC cancels upcoming 'Bachelorette' season, citing video of star in 2023 attack
item.description: ABC is pulling the 22nd season of 'The Bachelorette' days ahead of its scheduled release after TMZ published a 2023 video of its star, Frankie Taylor Paul, attacking her ex-boyfriend Dakota Mortensen.
item.content:encoded: ABC is pulling the 22nd season of 'The Bachelorette' days ahead of its scheduled release after TMZ published a 2023 video of its star, Frankie Taylor Paul, attacking her ex-boyfriend Dakota Mortensen.
item.dc:creator: Chloe Veltman

item.title: Activist on reactions from the Latino community to Cesar Chavez abuse allegations
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks activist and author Mónica Ramírez about reaction within the Latino community to the allegations of sexual violence against Cesar Chavez.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin asks activist and author Mónica Ramírez about reaction within the Latino community to the allegations of sexual violence against Cesar Chavez.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Amid war in Iran, Iranian Americans find ways to bring in Persian New Year with hope
item.description: As war in the Middle East escalates, communities across the U.S. ponder how to mark Persian New Year's Day, or Nowruz.
item.content:encoded: As war in the Middle East escalates, communities across the U.S. ponder how to mark Persian New Year's Day, or Nowruz.
item.dc:creator: Hosts
